steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The researcher argues that GPS-tracked distance is significantly higher than the RTD method for the same animal. The correct choice must pair a GPS value with an RTD value for the same caribou so that the GPS value is the larger one, directly supporting that claim.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — ‘caribou would be greater than 2,155 kilometers if the RTD method had been used.’: This reverses the researcher's argument. ✗
  • Choice B — ‘caribou would be less than 3,807 kilometers if the RTD method had been used.’: If the GPS-measured distance is $3{,}807$ km and the RTD value for the same caribou is less, that directly supports the claim that GPS records more distance. ✓
  • Choice C — ‘white-eared kob would be less than 400 kilometers if the GPS method had been used.’: Cross-animal comparisons don't isolate the effect of the measurement method. ✗
  • Choice D — ‘white-eared kob would be greater than 3,807 kilometers if the GPS method had been used.’: Starting from the RTD value and predicting a GPS outcome doesn't follow logically from the argument as stated. ✗
That confirms B is correct.
